category|Category:Meta-jinja}} [[EN:Meta Shrine]] [[JA:賣太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11635096}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Meta Shrine |Image = [[File:Meta Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213}} 319 Hieda-chō |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates2DegreesMinutesSeconds|34|38|21|N|135|47|46|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Hieda no Are|Q164522}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded=Unknown |Main hall={{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |Annual festival={{jalink|October 19|Q2961}} |Rituals=Are Festival ({{jalink|May 5|Q2550}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Meta Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |}} '''Meta Shrine''' (めたじんじゃ, '''Urita Shrine''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located at the edge of the Hieda {{jalink|moated settlement|Q60995739}} in {{jalink|Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|prefectural shrine|Q712534|modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Hieda no Are|Q164522}}, who was involved in the compilation of the {{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}, as the principal deity (main enshrined deity), with {{jalink|Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} as secondary deities (associated deities). Hieda no Are is regarded as a deity of learning and storytelling. Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the progenitor deity of the performing arts. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto is a deity of land and directions, and the husband deity of Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto. Hieda no Are came from the Hieda clan of the {{jalink|Sarume no Kimi|Q11572091}}, and Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the {{jalink|ancestral deity|Q85882782}} and {{jalink|clan deity|Q767655}} of the Sarume no Kimi. In the Middle Ages, the enshrined deity was described as “unknown.” === History === Hieda was the home base of the Hieda clan of the Sarume no Kimi, who traced their ancestry to Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, and it is thought that the shrine was founded by the time of the Asuka period under Empress Suiko as an ancestral mausoleum-shrine. In the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, along the western edge of the Hieda settlement ran the “Shimotsu-michi,” a major arterial route usable by both land and water that connected to {{jalink|Heijō-kyō|Q862509}}. Because many ritual implements were excavated from there, it is believed that this shrine once stood near the {{jalink|Rajōmon|Q1344338}} of {{jalink|Heijō-kyō|Q862509}}, where it served to purify the defilement of people entering and leaving the capital and to pray for traffic safety, thus also functioning as a {{jalink|Dōsojin|Q269515}} (roadside guardian deity). It is recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as “{{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}{{jalink|Soekami District|Q7553491}} Meta Shrine,” and is listed as a minor shrine. As for the origin of the shrine’s name, a widely accepted theory holds that the Sarume no Kimi were granted many cultivated fields by the emperor, which were called Sarume fields, and their owners were called Sarumeta-nushi. Later, the character for “saru” (monkey) was abbreviated, and they came to be called Onna-ta-nushi, and the shrine that enshrined their ancestral deity came to be called “Meta Shrine.” Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called “Sansha Myōjin.” In {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 7) it became “Jūsansha Myōjin,” in {{jalink|1891|Q7837}} (Meiji 24) it was renamed “Urita Shrine,” and in {{jalink|1942|Q18625}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 17) it was renamed to its current name, “Meta Shrine.” === Festivals === === Are Festival === The Are Festival (Areisai) is a festival commemorating the virtues of Hieda no Are. It was begun in {{jalink|1930|Q18787}} (Shōwa 5) by children’s literature author {{jalink|Takehiko Kurushima|Q6447506}}, who, with the cooperation of folktale writers from across the country, proposed that Hieda no Are was most fitting as a “god of stories” comparable to {{jalink|Hans Christian Andersen|Q5673|Andersen}}. After the offering of the Hieda dance, local children dedicate the Are-sama Ondo and the Are-sama Festival Children’s Song. Storytelling and read-aloud sessions of fairy tales are also held. On April 30, {{jalink|2012|Q1990}} (Heisei 24), a celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are was held to commemorate the 1,300th anniversary of the compilation of the {{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}{{jalink|compilation|Q11607994}}.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120430230832/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/120430/art12043016350003-n1.htm Celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are for the 1,300th anniversary of the Kojiki compilation] Sankei Shimbun, accessed April 30, 2012. It had long been held every year on {{jalink|August 16|Q2812}}, but in consideration of recent {{jalink|summer|Q1313}} {{jalink|extreme heat|Q3774617}}, it was decided that starting in {{jalink|2025|Q49616}} ({{jalink|Reiwa|Q62609017}} 7) it would be changed to {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} ({{jalink|Children’s Day|Q1145630}}).{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-19 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20250318/k00/00m/040/150000c |title=The Are Festival in Nara, associated with the Kojiki, succumbs to extreme heat: moved from August to May |website={{jalink|Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866}} Digital |publisher={{jalink|Mainichi Newspapers Co.|Q5358893}} |accessdate=2025-03-19}}{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-23 |url=https://www.yk-kankou.jp/newsDetail434.html |title=Regarding the holding of the 96th “Are Festival” |publisher=Yamatokoriyama City Tourism Association |accessdate=2025-03-29}}. === Facilities === * Kasuga Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Yabashira Shrine * Monument of the Storyteller === Notes === {{commons|Category:Meta-jinja}} {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Meta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Meta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:稗田阿礼]] [[Category:稗田氏]] [[Category:春日移し]]

category|Category:Meta-jinja}} [[EN:Meta Shrine]] [[JA:賣太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11635096}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Meta Shrine |Image = [[File:Meta Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213}} 319 Hieda-chō |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates2DegreesMinutesSeconds|34|38|21|N|135|47|46|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522}} |Shrine rank={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Unknown |Main hall={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961}} |Rituals=Are Festival ({{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Meta Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |}} '''Meta Shrine''' (めたじんじゃ, '''Urita Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located at the edge of the Hieda {{ill|lt=moated settlement|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739}} in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522}}, who was involved in the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}, as the principal deity (main enshrined deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} as secondary deities (associated deities). Hieda no Are is regarded as a deity of learning and storytelling. Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the progenitor deity of the performing arts. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto is a deity of land and directions, and the husband deity of Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto. Hieda no Are came from the Hieda clan of the {{ill|lt=Sarume no Kimi|Q11572091|WD=Q11572091}}, and Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}} and {{ill|lt=clan deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the Sarume no Kimi. In the Middle Ages, the enshrined deity was described as “unknown.” === History === Hieda was the home base of the Hieda clan of the Sarume no Kimi, who traced their ancestry to Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, and it is thought that the shrine was founded by the time of the Asuka period under Empress Suiko as an ancestral mausoleum-shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, along the western edge of the Hieda settlement ran the “Shimotsu-michi,” a major arterial route usable by both land and water that connected to {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}. Because many ritual implements were excavated from there, it is believed that this shrine once stood near the {{ill|lt=Rajōmon|Q1344338|WD=Q1344338}} of {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}, where it served to purify the defilement of people entering and leaving the capital and to pray for traffic safety, thus also functioning as a {{ill|lt=Dōsojin|Q269515|WD=Q269515}} (roadside guardian deity). It is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491}} Meta Shrine,” and is listed as a minor shrine. As for the origin of the shrine’s name, a widely accepted theory holds that the Sarume no Kimi were granted many cultivated fields by the emperor, which were called Sarume fields, and their owners were called Sarumeta-nushi. Later, the character for “saru” (monkey) was abbreviated, and they came to be called Onna-ta-nushi, and the shrine that enshrined their ancestral deity came to be called “Meta Shrine.” Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called “Sansha Myōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 7) it became “Jūsansha Myōjin,” in {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837}} (Meiji 24) it was renamed “Urita Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 17) it was renamed to its current name, “Meta Shrine.” === Festivals === === Are Festival === The Are Festival (Areisai) is a festival commemorating the virtues of Hieda no Are. It was begun in {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787}} (Shōwa 5) by children’s literature author {{ill|lt=Takehiko Kurushima|Q6447506|WD=Q6447506}}, who, with the cooperation of folktale writers from across the country, proposed that Hieda no Are was most fitting as a “god of stories” comparable to {{ill|lt=Hans Christian Andersen|Q5673|WD=Q5673|lt=Andersen}}. After the offering of the Hieda dance, local children dedicate the Are-sama Ondo and the Are-sama Festival Children’s Song. Storytelling and read-aloud sessions of fairy tales are also held. On April 30, {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}} (Heisei 24), a celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are was held to commemorate the 1,300th anniversary of the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}{{ill|lt=compilation|Q11607994|WD=Q11607994}}.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120430230832/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/120430/art12043016350003-n1.htm Celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are for the 1,300th anniversary of the Kojiki compilation] Sankei Shimbun, accessed April 30, 2012. It had long been held every year on {{ill|lt=August 16|Q2812|WD=Q2812}}, but in consideration of recent {{ill|lt=summer|Q1313|WD=Q1313}} {{ill|lt=extreme heat|Q3774617|WD=Q3774617}}, it was decided that starting in {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017}} 7) it would be changed to {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}} ({{ill|lt=Children’s Day|Q1145630|WD=Q1145630}}).{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-19 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20250318/k00/00m/040/150000c |title=The Are Festival in Nara, associated with the Kojiki, succumbs to extreme heat: moved from August to May |website={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} Digital |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Newspapers Co.|Q5358893|WD=Q5358893}} |accessdate=2025-03-19}}{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-23 |url=https://www.yk-kankou.jp/newsDetail434.html |title=Regarding the holding of the 96th “Are Festival” |publisher=Yamatokoriyama City Tourism Association |accessdate=2025-03-29}}. === Facilities === * Kasuga Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Yabashira Shrine * Monument of the Storyteller === Notes === {{commons|Category:Meta-jinja}} {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Meta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Meta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:稗田阿礼]] [[Category:稗田氏]] [[Category:春日移し]]

category|Category:Meta-jinja}} [[EN:Meta Shrine]] [[JA:賣太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11635096}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Meta Shrine |Image = [[File:Meta Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213}} 319 Hieda-chō |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates2DegreesMinutesSeconds|34|38|21|N|135|47|46|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Unknown |Main hall={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961}} |Rituals=Are Festival ({{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Meta Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |}} '''Meta Shrine''' (めたじんじゃ, '''Urita Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located at the edge of the Hieda {{ill|lt=moated settlement|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739}} in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522}}, who was involved in the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}, as the principal deity (main enshrined deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} as secondary deities (associated deities). Hieda no Are is regarded as a deity of learning and storytelling. Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the progenitor deity of the performing arts. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto is a deity of land and directions, and the husband deity of Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto. Hieda no Are came from the Hieda clan of the {{ill|lt=Sarume no Kimi|Q11572091|WD=Q11572091}}, and Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}} and {{ill|lt=clan deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the Sarume no Kimi. In the Middle Ages, the enshrined deity was described as “unknown.” === History === Hieda was the home base of the Hieda clan of the Sarume no Kimi, who traced their ancestry to Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, and it is thought that the shrine was founded by the time of the Asuka period under Empress Suiko as an ancestral mausoleum-shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, along the western edge of the Hieda settlement ran the “Shimotsu-michi,” a major arterial route usable by both land and water that connected to {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}. Because many ritual implements were excavated from there, it is believed that this shrine once stood near the {{ill|lt=Rajōmon|Q1344338|WD=Q1344338}} of {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}, where it served to purify the defilement of people entering and leaving the capital and to pray for traffic safety, thus also functioning as a {{ill|lt=Dōsojin|Q269515|WD=Q269515}} (roadside guardian deity). It is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491}} Meta Shrine,” and is listed as a minor shrine. As for the origin of the shrine’s name, a widely accepted theory holds that the Sarume no Kimi were granted many cultivated fields by the emperor, which were called Sarume fields, and their owners were called Sarumeta-nushi. Later, the character for “saru” (monkey) was abbreviated, and they came to be called Onna-ta-nushi, and the shrine that enshrined their ancestral deity came to be called “Meta Shrine.” Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called “Sansha Myōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 7) it became “Jūsansha Myōjin,” in {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837}} (Meiji 24) it was renamed “Urita Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 17) it was renamed to its current name, “Meta Shrine.” === Festivals === === Are Festival === The Are Festival (Areisai) is a festival commemorating the virtues of Hieda no Are. It was begun in {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787}} (Shōwa 5) by children’s literature author {{ill|lt=Takehiko Kurushima|Q6447506|WD=Q6447506}}, who, with the cooperation of folktale writers from across the country, proposed that Hieda no Are was most fitting as a “god of stories” comparable to {{ill|lt=Hans Christian Andersen|Q5673|WD=Q5673|lt=Andersen}}. After the offering of the Hieda dance, local children dedicate the Are-sama Ondo and the Are-sama Festival Children’s Song. Storytelling and read-aloud sessions of fairy tales are also held. On April 30, {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}} (Heisei 24), a celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are was held to commemorate the 1,300th anniversary of the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}{{ill|lt=compilation|Q11607994|WD=Q11607994}}.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120430230832/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/120430/art12043016350003-n1.htm Celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are for the 1,300th anniversary of the Kojiki compilation] Sankei Shimbun, accessed April 30, 2012. It had long been held every year on {{ill|lt=August 16|Q2812|WD=Q2812}}, but in consideration of recent {{ill|lt=summer|Q1313|WD=Q1313}} {{ill|lt=extreme heat|Q3774617|WD=Q3774617}}, it was decided that starting in {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017}} 7) it would be changed to {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}} ({{ill|lt=Children’s Day|Q1145630|WD=Q1145630}}).{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-19 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20250318/k00/00m/040/150000c |title=The Are Festival in Nara, associated with the Kojiki, succumbs to extreme heat: moved from August to May |website={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} Digital |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Newspapers Co.|Q5358893|WD=Q5358893}} |accessdate=2025-03-19}}{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-23 |url=https://www.yk-kankou.jp/newsDetail434.html |title=Regarding the holding of the 96th “Are Festival” |publisher=Yamatokoriyama City Tourism Association |accessdate=2025-03-29}}. === Facilities === * Kasuga Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Yabashira Shrine * Monument of the Storyteller === Notes === {{commons|Category:Meta-jinja}} {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Meta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Meta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:稗田阿礼]] [[Category:稗田氏]] [[Category:春日移し]]

Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Unknown |Main hall={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961}} |Rituals=Are Festival ({{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Meta Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |}} '''Meta Shrine''' (めたじんじゃ, '''Urita Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located at the edge of the Hieda {{ill|lt=moated settlement|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739}} in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522}}, who was involved in the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}, as the principal deity (main enshrined deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} as secondary deities (associated deities). Hieda no Are is regarded as a deity of learning and storytelling. Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the progenitor deity of the performing arts. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto is a deity of land and directions, and the husband deity of Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto. Hieda no Are came from the Hieda clan of the {{ill|lt=Sarume no Kimi|Q11572091|WD=Q11572091}}, and Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}} and {{ill|lt=clan deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the Sarume no Kimi. In the Middle Ages, the enshrined deity was described as “unknown.” === History === Hieda was the home base of the Hieda clan of the Sarume no Kimi, who traced their ancestry to Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, and it is thought that the shrine was founded by the time of the Asuka period under Empress Suiko as an ancestral mausoleum-shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, along the western edge of the Hieda settlement ran the “Shimotsu-michi,” a major arterial route usable by both land and water that connected to {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}. Because many ritual implements were excavated from there, it is believed that this shrine once stood near the {{ill|lt=Rajōmon|Q1344338|WD=Q1344338}} of {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}, where it served to purify the defilement of people entering and leaving the capital and to pray for traffic safety, thus also functioning as a {{ill|lt=Dōsojin|Q269515|WD=Q269515}} (roadside guardian deity). It is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491}} Meta Shrine,” and is listed as a minor shrine. As for the origin of the shrine’s name, a widely accepted theory holds that the Sarume no Kimi were granted many cultivated fields by the emperor, which were called Sarume fields, and their owners were called Sarumeta-nushi. Later, the character for “saru” (monkey) was abbreviated, and they came to be called Onna-ta-nushi, and the shrine that enshrined their ancestral deity came to be called “Meta Shrine.” Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called “Sansha Myōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 7) it became “Jūsansha Myōjin,” in {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837}} (Meiji 24) it was renamed “Urita Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 17) it was renamed to its current name, “Meta Shrine.” === Festivals === === Are Festival === The Are Festival (Areisai) is a festival commemorating the virtues of Hieda no Are. It was begun in {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787}} (Shōwa 5) by children’s literature author {{ill|lt=Takehiko Kurushima|Q6447506|WD=Q6447506}}, who, with the cooperation of folktale writers from across the country, proposed that Hieda no Are was most fitting as a “god of stories” comparable to {{ill|lt=Hans Christian Andersen|Q5673|WD=Q5673|lt=Andersen}}. After the offering of the Hieda dance, local children dedicate the Are-sama Ondo and the Are-sama Festival Children’s Song. Storytelling and read-aloud sessions of fairy tales are also held. On April 30, {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}} (Heisei 24), a celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are was held to commemorate the 1,300th anniversary of the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}{{ill|lt=compilation|Q11607994|WD=Q11607994}}.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120430230832/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/120430/art12043016350003-n1.htm Celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are for the 1,300th anniversary of the Kojiki compilation] Sankei Shimbun, accessed April 30, 2012. It had long been held every year on {{ill|lt=August 16|Q2812|WD=Q2812}}, but in consideration of recent {{ill|lt=summer|Q1313|WD=Q1313}} {{ill|lt=extreme heat|Q3774617|WD=Q3774617}}, it was decided that starting in {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017}} 7) it would be changed to {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}} ({{ill|lt=Children’s Day|Q1145630|WD=Q1145630}}).{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-19 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20250318/k00/00m/040/150000c |title=The Are Festival in Nara, associated with the Kojiki, succumbs to extreme heat: moved from August to May |website={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} Digital |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Newspapers Co.|Q5358893|WD=Q5358893}} |accessdate=2025-03-19}}{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-23 |url=https://www.yk-kankou.jp/newsDetail434.html |title=Regarding the holding of the 96th “Are Festival” |publisher=Yamatokoriyama City Tourism Association |accessdate=2025-03-29}}. === Facilities === * Kasuga Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Yabashira Shrine * Monument of the Storyteller === Notes === {{commons|Category:Meta-jinja}} {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Meta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Meta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:稗田阿礼]] [[Category:稗田氏]] [[Category:春日移し]]

category|Category:Meta-jinja}} [[EN:Meta Shrine]] [[JA:賣太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11635096}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Meta Shrine |Image = [[File:Meta Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213}} 319 Hieda-chō |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates2DegreesMinutesSeconds|34|38|21|N|135|47|46|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Unknown |Main hall={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961}} |Rituals=Are Festival ({{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Meta Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |}} '''Meta Shrine''' (めたじんじゃ, '''Urita Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located at the edge of the Hieda {{ill|lt=moated settlement|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739}} in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522}}, who was involved in the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}, as the principal deity (main enshrined deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} as secondary deities (associated deities). Hieda no Are is regarded as a deity of learning and storytelling. Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the progenitor deity of the performing arts. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto is a deity of land and directions, and the husband deity of Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto. Hieda no Are came from the Hieda clan of the {{ill|lt=Sarume no Kimi|Q11572091|WD=Q11572091}}, and Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}} and {{ill|lt=clan deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the Sarume no Kimi. In the Middle Ages, the enshrined deity was described as “unknown.” === History === Hieda was the home base of the Hieda clan of the Sarume no Kimi, who traced their ancestry to Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, and it is thought that the shrine was founded by the time of the Asuka period under Empress Suiko as an ancestral mausoleum-shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, along the western edge of the Hieda settlement ran the “Shimotsu-michi,” a major arterial route usable by both land and water that connected to {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}. Because many ritual implements were excavated from there, it is believed that this shrine once stood near the {{ill|lt=Rajōmon|Q1344338|WD=Q1344338}} of {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}, where it served to purify the defilement of people entering and leaving the capital and to pray for traffic safety, thus also functioning as a {{ill|lt=Dōsojin|Q269515|WD=Q269515}} (roadside guardian deity). It is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491}} Meta Shrine,” and is listed as a minor shrine. As for the origin of the shrine’s name, a widely accepted theory holds that the Sarume no Kimi were granted many cultivated fields by the emperor, which were called Sarume fields, and their owners were called Sarumeta-nushi. Later, the character for “saru” (monkey) was abbreviated, and they came to be called Onna-ta-nushi, and the shrine that enshrined their ancestral deity came to be called “Meta Shrine.” Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called “Sansha Myōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 7) it became “Jūsansha Myōjin,” in {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837}} (Meiji 24) it was renamed “Urita Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 17) it was renamed to its current name, “Meta Shrine.” === Festivals === === Are Festival === The Are Festival (Areisai) is a festival commemorating the virtues of Hieda no Are. It was begun in {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787}} (Shōwa 5) by children’s literature author {{ill|lt=Takehiko Kurushima|Q6447506|WD=Q6447506}}, who, with the cooperation of folktale writers from across the country, proposed that Hieda no Are was most fitting as a “god of stories” comparable to {{ill|lt=Hans Christian Andersen|Q5673|WD=Q5673|lt=Andersen}}. After the offering of the Hieda dance, local children dedicate the Are-sama Ondo and the Are-sama Festival Children’s Song. Storytelling and read-aloud sessions of fairy tales are also held. On April 30, {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}} (Heisei 24), a celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are was held to commemorate the 1,300th anniversary of the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}{{ill|lt=compilation|Q11607994|WD=Q11607994}}.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120430230832/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/120430/art12043016350003-n1.htm Celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are for the 1,300th anniversary of the Kojiki compilation] Sankei Shimbun, accessed April 30, 2012. It had long been held every year on {{ill|lt=August 16|Q2812|WD=Q2812}}, but in consideration of recent {{ill|lt=summer|Q1313|WD=Q1313}} {{ill|lt=extreme heat|Q3774617|WD=Q3774617}}, it was decided that starting in {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017}} 7) it would be changed to {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}} ({{ill|lt=Children’s Day|Q1145630|WD=Q1145630}}).{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-19 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20250318/k00/00m/040/150000c |title=The Are Festival in Nara, associated with the Kojiki, succumbs to extreme heat: moved from August to May |website={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} Digital |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Newspapers Co.|Q5358893|WD=Q5358893}} |accessdate=2025-03-19}}{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-23 |url=https://www.yk-kankou.jp/newsDetail434.html |title=Regarding the holding of the 96th “Are Festival” |publisher=Yamatokoriyama City Tourism Association |accessdate=2025-03-29}}. === Facilities === * Kasuga Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Yabashira Shrine * Monument of the Storyteller === Notes === {{commons|Category:Meta-jinja}} {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Meta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Meta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:稗田阿礼]] [[Category:稗田氏]] [[Category:春日移し]]

category|Category:Meta-jinja}} [[EN:Meta Shrine]] [[JA:賣太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11635096}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Meta Shrine |Image = [[File:Meta Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213}} 319 Hieda-chō |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates2DegreesMinutesSeconds|34|38|21|N|135|47|46|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Unknown |Main hall={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961}} |Rituals=Are Festival ({{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Meta Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |}} '''Meta Shrine''' (めたじんじゃ, '''Urita Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located at the edge of the Hieda {{ill|lt=moated settlement|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739}} in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522}}, who was involved in the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}, as the principal deity (main enshrined deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} as secondary deities (associated deities). Hieda no Are is regarded as a deity of learning and storytelling. Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the progenitor deity of the performing arts. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto is a deity of land and directions, and the husband deity of Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto. Hieda no Are came from the Hieda clan of the {{ill|lt=Sarume no Kimi|Q11572091|WD=Q11572091}}, and Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}} and {{ill|lt=clan deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the Sarume no Kimi. In the Middle Ages, the enshrined deity was described as “unknown.” === History === Hieda was the home base of the Hieda clan of the Sarume no Kimi, who traced their ancestry to Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, and it is thought that the shrine was founded by the time of the Asuka period under Empress Suiko as an ancestral mausoleum-shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, along the western edge of the Hieda settlement ran the “Shimotsu-michi,” a major arterial route usable by both land and water that connected to {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}. Because many ritual implements were excavated from there, it is believed that this shrine once stood near the {{ill|lt=Rajōmon|Q1344338|WD=Q1344338}} of {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}, where it served to purify the defilement of people entering and leaving the capital and to pray for traffic safety, thus also functioning as a {{ill|lt=Dōsojin|Q269515|WD=Q269515}} (roadside guardian deity). It is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491}} Meta Shrine,” and is listed as a minor shrine. As for the origin of the shrine’s name, a widely accepted theory holds that the Sarume no Kimi were granted many cultivated fields by the emperor, which were called Sarume fields, and their owners were called Sarumeta-nushi. Later, the character for “saru” (monkey) was abbreviated, and they came to be called Onna-ta-nushi, and the shrine that enshrined their ancestral deity came to be called “Meta Shrine.” Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called “Sansha Myōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 7) it became “Jūsansha Myōjin,” in {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837}} (Meiji 24) it was renamed “Urita Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 17) it was renamed to its current name, “Meta Shrine.” === Festivals === === Are Festival === The Are Festival (Areisai) is a festival commemorating the virtues of Hieda no Are. It was begun in {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787}} (Shōwa 5) by children’s literature author {{ill|lt=Takehiko Kurushima|Q6447506|WD=Q6447506}}, who, with the cooperation of folktale writers from across the country, proposed that Hieda no Are was most fitting as a “god of stories” comparable to {{ill|lt=Hans Christian Andersen|Q5673|WD=Q5673|lt=Andersen}}. After the offering of the Hieda dance, local children dedicate the Are-sama Ondo and the Are-sama Festival Children’s Song. Storytelling and read-aloud sessions of fairy tales are also held. On April 30, {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}} (Heisei 24), a celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are was held to commemorate the 1,300th anniversary of the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}{{ill|lt=compilation|Q11607994|WD=Q11607994}}.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120430230832/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/120430/art12043016350003-n1.htm Celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are for the 1,300th anniversary of the Kojiki compilation] Sankei Shimbun, accessed April 30, 2012. It had long been held every year on {{ill|lt=August 16|Q2812|WD=Q2812}}, but in consideration of recent {{ill|lt=summer|Q1313|WD=Q1313}} {{ill|lt=extreme heat|Q3774617|WD=Q3774617}}, it was decided that starting in {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017}} 7) it would be changed to {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}} ({{ill|lt=Children’s Day|Q1145630|WD=Q1145630}}).{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-19 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20250318/k00/00m/040/150000c |title=The Are Festival in Nara, associated with the Kojiki, succumbs to extreme heat: moved from August to May |website={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} Digital |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Newspapers Co.|Q5358893|WD=Q5358893}} |accessdate=2025-03-19}}{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-23 |url=https://www.yk-kankou.jp/newsDetail434.html |title=Regarding the holding of the 96th “Are Festival” |publisher=Yamatokoriyama City Tourism Association |accessdate=2025-03-29}}. === Facilities === * Kasuga Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Yabashira Shrine * Monument of the Storyteller === Notes === {{commons|Category:Meta-jinja}} {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Meta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Meta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:稗田阿礼]] [[Category:稗田氏]] [[Category:春日移し]]

category|Category:Meta-jinja}} [[EN:Meta Shrine]] [[JA:賣太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11635096}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Meta Shrine |Image = [[File:Meta Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213}} 319 Hieda-chō |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates2DegreesMinutesSeconds|34|38|21|N|135|47|46|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Unknown |Main hall={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961}} |Rituals=Are Festival ({{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Meta Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |}} '''Meta Shrine''' (めたじんじゃ, '''Urita Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located at the edge of the Hieda {{ill|lt=moated settlement|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739}} in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522}}, who was involved in the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}, as the principal deity (main enshrined deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} as secondary deities (associated deities). Hieda no Are is regarded as a deity of learning and storytelling. Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the progenitor deity of the performing arts. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto is a deity of land and directions, and the husband deity of Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto. Hieda no Are came from the Hieda clan of the {{ill|lt=Sarume no Kimi|Q11572091|WD=Q11572091}}, and Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}} and {{ill|lt=clan deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the Sarume no Kimi. In the Middle Ages, the enshrined deity was described as “unknown.” === History === Hieda was the home base of the Hieda clan of the Sarume no Kimi, who traced their ancestry to Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, and it is thought that the shrine was founded by the time of the Asuka period under Empress Suiko as an ancestral mausoleum-shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, along the western edge of the Hieda settlement ran the “Shimotsu-michi,” a major arterial route usable by both land and water that connected to {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}. Because many ritual implements were excavated from there, it is believed that this shrine once stood near the {{ill|lt=Rajōmon|Q1344338|WD=Q1344338}} of {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509}}, where it served to purify the defilement of people entering and leaving the capital and to pray for traffic safety, thus also functioning as a {{ill|lt=Dōsojin|Q269515|WD=Q269515}} (roadside guardian deity). It is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491}} Meta Shrine,” and is listed as a minor shrine. As for the origin of the shrine’s name, a widely accepted theory holds that the Sarume no Kimi were granted many cultivated fields by the emperor, which were called Sarume fields, and their owners were called Sarumeta-nushi. Later, the character for “saru” (monkey) was abbreviated, and they came to be called Onna-ta-nushi, and the shrine that enshrined their ancestral deity came to be called “Meta Shrine.” Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called “Sansha Myōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 7) it became “Jūsansha Myōjin,” in {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837}} (Meiji 24) it was renamed “Urita Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 17) it was renamed to its current name, “Meta Shrine.” === Festivals === === Are Festival === The Are Festival (Areisai) is a festival commemorating the virtues of Hieda no Are. It was begun in {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787}} (Shōwa 5) by children’s literature author {{ill|lt=Takehiko Kurushima|Q6447506|WD=Q6447506}}, who, with the cooperation of folktale writers from across the country, proposed that Hieda no Are was most fitting as a “god of stories” comparable to {{ill|lt=Hans Christian Andersen|Q5673|WD=Q5673|lt=Andersen}}. After the offering of the Hieda dance, local children dedicate the Are-sama Ondo and the Are-sama Festival Children’s Song. Storytelling and read-aloud sessions of fairy tales are also held. On April 30, {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990}} (Heisei 24), a celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are was held to commemorate the 1,300th anniversary of the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}{{ill|lt=compilation|Q11607994|WD=Q11607994}}.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120430230832/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/120430/art12043016350003-n1.htm Celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are for the 1,300th anniversary of the Kojiki compilation] Sankei Shimbun, accessed April 30, 2012. It had long been held every year on {{ill|lt=August 16|Q2812|WD=Q2812}}, but in consideration of recent {{ill|lt=summer|Q1313|WD=Q1313}} {{ill|lt=extreme heat|Q3774617|WD=Q3774617}}, it was decided that starting in {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017}} 7) it would be changed to {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}} ({{ill|lt=Children’s Day|Q1145630|WD=Q1145630}}).{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-19 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20250318/k00/00m/040/150000c |title=The Are Festival in Nara, associated with the Kojiki, succumbs to extreme heat: moved from August to May |website={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866}} Digital |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Newspapers Co.|Q5358893|WD=Q5358893}} |accessdate=2025-03-19}}{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-23 |url=https://www.yk-kankou.jp/newsDetail434.html |title=Regarding the holding of the 96th “Are Festival” |publisher=Yamatokoriyama City Tourism Association |accessdate=2025-03-29}}. === Facilities === * Kasuga Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Yabashira Shrine * Monument of the Storyteller === Notes === {{commons|Category:Meta-jinja}} {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Meta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Meta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:稗田阿礼]] [[Category:稗田氏]] [[Category:春日移し]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8150005002795 == Commons category (P373) == * Meta-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市稗田町319', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Meta-jinja}} [[EN:Meta Shrine]] [[JA:賣太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11635096}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Meta Shrine |Image = [[File:Meta Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}} 319 Hieda-chō |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates2DegreesMinutesSeconds|34|38|21|N|135|47|46|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522|1=Hieda no Are}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded=Unknown |Main hall={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} |Rituals=Are Festival ({{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Meta Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |}} '''Meta Shrine''' (めたじんじゃ, '''Urita Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located at the edge of the Hieda {{ill|lt=moated settlement|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739|1=Moated settlements}} in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522|1=Hieda no Are}}, who was involved in the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}, as the principal deity (main enshrined deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} and {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} as secondary deities (associated deities). Hieda no Are is regarded as a deity of learning and storytelling. Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the progenitor deity of the performing arts. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto is a deity of land and directions, and the husband deity of Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto. Hieda no Are came from the Hieda clan of the {{ill|lt=Sarume no Kimi|Q11572091|WD=Q11572091|1=Sarume clan}}, and Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} and {{ill|lt=clan deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Sarume no Kimi. In the Middle Ages, the enshrined deity was described as “unknown.” === History === Hieda was the home base of the Hieda clan of the Sarume no Kimi, who traced their ancestry to Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, and it is thought that the shrine was founded by the time of the Asuka period under Empress Suiko as an ancestral mausoleum-shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, along the western edge of the Hieda settlement ran the “Shimotsu-michi,” a major arterial route usable by both land and water that connected to {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509|1=Heijō-kyō}}. Because many ritual implements were excavated from there, it is believed that this shrine once stood near the {{ill|lt=Rajōmon|Q1344338|WD=Q1344338|1=Rajōmon}} of {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509|1=Heijō-kyō}}, where it served to purify the defilement of people entering and leaving the capital and to pray for traffic safety, thus also functioning as a {{ill|lt=Dōsojin|Q269515|WD=Q269515|1=Dōsojin}} (roadside guardian deity). It is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491|1=Soekami District, Nara}} Meta Shrine,” and is listed as a minor shrine. As for the origin of the shrine’s name, a widely accepted theory holds that the Sarume no Kimi were granted many cultivated fields by the emperor, which were called Sarume fields, and their owners were called Sarumeta-nushi. Later, the character for “saru” (monkey) was abbreviated, and they came to be called Onna-ta-nushi, and the shrine that enshrined their ancestral deity came to be called “Meta Shrine.” Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called “Sansha Myōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7) it became “Jūsansha Myōjin,” in {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837|1=1891}} (Meiji 24) it was renamed “Urita Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625|1=1942}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 17) it was renamed to its current name, “Meta Shrine.” === Festivals === === Are Festival === The Are Festival (Areisai) is a festival commemorating the virtues of Hieda no Are. It was begun in {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}} (Shōwa 5) by children’s literature author {{ill|lt=Takehiko Kurushima|Q6447506|WD=Q6447506|1=Kurushima Takehiko}}, who, with the cooperation of folktale writers from across the country, proposed that Hieda no Are was most fitting as a “god of stories” comparable to {{ill|lt=Hans Christian Andersen|Q5673|WD=Q5673|lt=Andersen|1=Hans Christian Andersen}}. After the offering of the Hieda dance, local children dedicate the Are-sama Ondo and the Are-sama Festival Children’s Song. Storytelling and read-aloud sessions of fairy tales are also held. On April 30, {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}} (Heisei 24), a celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are was held to commemorate the 1,300th anniversary of the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}{{ill|lt=compilation|Q11607994|WD=Q11607994|1=compilation}}.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120430230832/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/120430/art12043016350003-n1.htm Celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are for the 1,300th anniversary of the Kojiki compilation] Sankei Shimbun, accessed April 30, 2012. It had long been held every year on {{ill|lt=August 16|Q2812|WD=Q2812|1=August 16}}, but in consideration of recent {{ill|lt=summer|Q1313|WD=Q1313|1=Summer}} {{ill|lt=extreme heat|Q3774617|WD=Q3774617|1=extreme heat}}, it was decided that starting in {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 7) it would be changed to {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} ({{ill|lt=Children’s Day|Q1145630|WD=Q1145630|1=Children's Day (Japan)}}).{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-19 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20250318/k00/00m/040/150000c |title=The Are Festival in Nara, associated with the Kojiki, succumbs to extreme heat: moved from August to May |website={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} Digital |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Newspapers Co.|Q5358893|WD=Q5358893|1=The Mainichi Newspapers Co.}} |accessdate=2025-03-19}}{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-23 |url=https://www.yk-kankou.jp/newsDetail434.html |title=Regarding the holding of the 96th “Are Festival” |publisher=Yamatokoriyama City Tourism Association |accessdate=2025-03-29}}. === Facilities === * Kasuga Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Yabashira Shrine * Monument of the Storyteller === Notes === {{commons|Category:Meta-jinja}} {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Meta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Meta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:稗田阿礼]] [[Category:稗田氏]] [[Category:春日移し]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8150005002795 == Commons category (P373) == * Meta-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市稗田町319', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Meta-jinja}} [[EN:Meta Shrine]] [[JA:賣太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11635096}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Meta Shrine |Image = [[File:Meta Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}} 319 Hieda-chō |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates2DegreesMinutesSeconds|34|38|21|N|135|47|46|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522|1=Hieda no Are}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded=Unknown |Main hall={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} |Rituals=Are Festival ({{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Meta Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |}} '''Meta Shrine''' (めたじんじゃ, '''Urita Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located at the edge of the Hieda {{ill|lt=moated settlement|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739|1=Moated settlements}} in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522|1=Hieda no Are}}, who was involved in the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}, as the principal deity (main enshrined deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} and {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} as secondary deities (associated deities). Hieda no Are is regarded as a deity of learning and storytelling. Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the progenitor deity of the performing arts. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto is a deity of land and directions, and the husband deity of Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto. Hieda no Are came from the Hieda clan of the {{ill|lt=Sarume no Kimi|Q11572091|WD=Q11572091|1=Sarume clan}}, and Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} and {{ill|lt=clan deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Sarume no Kimi. In the Middle Ages, the enshrined deity was described as “unknown.” === History === Hieda was the home base of the Hieda clan of the Sarume no Kimi, who traced their ancestry to Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, and it is thought that the shrine was founded by the time of the Asuka period under Empress Suiko as an ancestral mausoleum-shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, along the western edge of the Hieda settlement ran the “Shimotsu-michi,” a major arterial route usable by both land and water that connected to {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509|1=Heijō-kyō}}. Because many ritual implements were excavated from there, it is believed that this shrine once stood near the {{ill|lt=Rajōmon|Q1344338|WD=Q1344338|1=Rajōmon}} of {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509|1=Heijō-kyō}}, where it served to purify the defilement of people entering and leaving the capital and to pray for traffic safety, thus also functioning as a {{ill|lt=Dōsojin|Q269515|WD=Q269515|1=Dōsojin}} (roadside guardian deity). It is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491|1=Soekami District, Nara}} Meta Shrine,” and is listed as a minor shrine. As for the origin of the shrine’s name, a widely accepted theory holds that the Sarume no Kimi were granted many cultivated fields by the emperor, which were called Sarume fields, and their owners were called Sarumeta-nushi. Later, the character for “saru” (monkey) was abbreviated, and they came to be called Onna-ta-nushi, and the shrine that enshrined their ancestral deity came to be called “Meta Shrine.” Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called “Sansha Myōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7) it became “Jūsansha Myōjin,” in {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837|1=1891}} (Meiji 24) it was renamed “Urita Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625|1=1942}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 17) it was renamed to its current name, “Meta Shrine.” === Festivals === === Are Festival === The Are Festival (Areisai) is a festival commemorating the virtues of Hieda no Are. It was begun in {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}} (Shōwa 5) by children’s literature author {{ill|lt=Takehiko Kurushima|Q6447506|WD=Q6447506|1=Kurushima Takehiko}}, who, with the cooperation of folktale writers from across the country, proposed that Hieda no Are was most fitting as a “god of stories” comparable to {{ill|lt=Hans Christian Andersen|Q5673|WD=Q5673|lt=Andersen|1=Hans Christian Andersen}}. After the offering of the Hieda dance, local children dedicate the Are-sama Ondo and the Are-sama Festival Children’s Song. Storytelling and read-aloud sessions of fairy tales are also held. On April 30, {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}} (Heisei 24), a celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are was held to commemorate the 1,300th anniversary of the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}{{ill|lt=compilation|Q11607994|WD=Q11607994|1=compilation}}.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120430230832/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/120430/art12043016350003-n1.htm Celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are for the 1,300th anniversary of the Kojiki compilation] Sankei Shimbun, accessed April 30, 2012. It had long been held every year on {{ill|lt=August 16|Q2812|WD=Q2812|1=August 16}}, but in consideration of recent {{ill|lt=summer|Q1313|WD=Q1313|1=Summer}} {{ill|lt=extreme heat|Q3774617|WD=Q3774617|1=extreme heat}}, it was decided that starting in {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 7) it would be changed to {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} ({{ill|lt=Children’s Day|Q1145630|WD=Q1145630|1=Children's Day (Japan)}}).{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-19 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20250318/k00/00m/040/150000c |title=The Are Festival in Nara, associated with the Kojiki, succumbs to extreme heat: moved from August to May |website={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} Digital |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Newspapers Co.|Q5358893|WD=Q5358893|1=The Mainichi Newspapers Co.}} |accessdate=2025-03-19}}{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-23 |url=https://www.yk-kankou.jp/newsDetail434.html |title=Regarding the holding of the 96th “Are Festival” |publisher=Yamatokoriyama City Tourism Association |accessdate=2025-03-29}}. === Facilities === * Kasuga Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Yabashira Shrine * Monument of the Storyteller === Notes === {{commons|Category:Meta-jinja}} {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Meta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Meta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:稗田阿礼]] [[Category:稗田氏]] [[Category:春日移し]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8150005002795 == Commons category (P373) == * Meta-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市稗田町319', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Meta-jinja}} [[EN:Meta Shrine]] [[JA:賣太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11635096}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Meta Shrine |Image = [[File:Meta Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}} 319 Hieda-chō |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates2DegreesMinutesSeconds|34|38|21|N|135|47|46|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522|1=Hieda no Are}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded=Unknown |Main hall={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|WD=Q2961|1=October 19}} |Rituals=Are Festival ({{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Meta Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} |}} '''Meta Shrine''' (めたじんじゃ, '''Urita Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located at the edge of the Hieda {{ill|lt=moated settlement|Q60995739|WD=Q60995739|1=Moated settlements}} in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Hieda no Are|Q164522|WD=Q164522|1=Hieda no Are}}, who was involved in the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}, as the principal deity (main enshrined deity), with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} and {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} as secondary deities (associated deities). Hieda no Are is regarded as a deity of learning and storytelling. Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the progenitor deity of the performing arts. Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto is a deity of land and directions, and the husband deity of Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto. Hieda no Are came from the Hieda clan of the {{ill|lt=Sarume no Kimi|Q11572091|WD=Q11572091|1=Sarume clan}}, and Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto is the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} and {{ill|lt=clan deity|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Sarume no Kimi. In the Middle Ages, the enshrined deity was described as “unknown.” === History === Hieda was the home base of the Hieda clan of the Sarume no Kimi, who traced their ancestry to Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto, and it is thought that the shrine was founded by the time of the Asuka period under Empress Suiko as an ancestral mausoleum-shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, along the western edge of the Hieda settlement ran the “Shimotsu-michi,” a major arterial route usable by both land and water that connected to {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509|1=Heijō-kyō}}. Because many ritual implements were excavated from there, it is believed that this shrine once stood near the {{ill|lt=Rajōmon|Q1344338|WD=Q1344338|1=Rajōmon}} of {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|WD=Q862509|1=Heijō-kyō}}, where it served to purify the defilement of people entering and leaving the capital and to pray for traffic safety, thus also functioning as a {{ill|lt=Dōsojin|Q269515|WD=Q269515|1=Dōsojin}} (roadside guardian deity). It is recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q7553491|WD=Q7553491|1=Soekami District, Nara}} Meta Shrine,” and is listed as a minor shrine. As for the origin of the shrine’s name, a widely accepted theory holds that the Sarume no Kimi were granted many cultivated fields by the emperor, which were called Sarume fields, and their owners were called Sarumeta-nushi. Later, the character for “saru” (monkey) was abbreviated, and they came to be called Onna-ta-nushi, and the shrine that enshrined their ancestral deity came to be called “Meta Shrine.” Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called “Sansha Myōjin.” In {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 7) it became “Jūsansha Myōjin,” in {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837|1=1891}} (Meiji 24) it was renamed “Urita Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625|1=1942}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 17) it was renamed to its current name, “Meta Shrine.” === Festivals === === Are Festival === The Are Festival (Areisai) is a festival commemorating the virtues of Hieda no Are. It was begun in {{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}} (Shōwa 5) by children’s literature author {{ill|lt=Takehiko Kurushima|Q6447506|WD=Q6447506|1=Kurushima Takehiko}}, who, with the cooperation of folktale writers from across the country, proposed that Hieda no Are was most fitting as a “god of stories” comparable to {{ill|lt=Hans Christian Andersen|Q5673|WD=Q5673|lt=Andersen|1=Hans Christian Andersen}}. After the offering of the Hieda dance, local children dedicate the Are-sama Ondo and the Are-sama Festival Children’s Song. Storytelling and read-aloud sessions of fairy tales are also held. On April 30, {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|WD=Q1990|1=2012}} (Heisei 24), a celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are was held to commemorate the 1,300th anniversary of the compilation of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}{{ill|lt=compilation|Q11607994|WD=Q11607994|1=compilation}}.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120430230832/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/120430/art12043016350003-n1.htm Celebratory festival honoring Hieda no Are for the 1,300th anniversary of the Kojiki compilation] Sankei Shimbun, accessed April 30, 2012. It had long been held every year on {{ill|lt=August 16|Q2812|WD=Q2812|1=August 16}}, but in consideration of recent {{ill|lt=summer|Q1313|WD=Q1313|1=Summer}} {{ill|lt=extreme heat|Q3774617|WD=Q3774617|1=extreme heat}}, it was decided that starting in {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 7) it would be changed to {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} ({{ill|lt=Children’s Day|Q1145630|WD=Q1145630|1=Children's Day (Japan)}}).{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-19 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20250318/k00/00m/040/150000c |title=The Are Festival in Nara, associated with the Kojiki, succumbs to extreme heat: moved from August to May |website={{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|WD=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}} Digital |publisher={{ill|lt=Mainichi Newspapers Co.|Q5358893|WD=Q5358893|1=The Mainichi Newspapers Co.}} |accessdate=2025-03-19}}{{Cite web |Japanese|date=2025-03-23 |url=https://www.yk-kankou.jp/newsDetail434.html |title=Regarding the holding of the 96th “Are Festival” |publisher=Yamatokoriyama City Tourism Association |accessdate=2025-03-29}}. === Facilities === * Kasuga Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Yabashira Shrine * Monument of the Storyteller === Notes === {{commons|Category:Meta-jinja}} {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Meta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Meta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:稗田阿礼]] [[Category:稗田氏]] [[Category:春日移し]]
